title: hedera helix var. canariensis
intro: Hedera helix var. canariensis, also known as the Canary Island ivy or Madeira ivy, is a variety of the common English ivy (Hedera helix) native to the Canary Islands and Madeira. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, derived from its saponins, flavonoids, and glycosides. The health benefits of Hedera helix var. canariensis include its potential to support cardiovascular health, improve cognitive function, and exhibit antiviral properties, making it a valuable herbal remedy. Hedera helix var. canariensis is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves can also be used in salves and ointments for topical applications.
